memoryArtifacts.headline: Family of Herbert from Annals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777
memoryArtifacts.description: Family of Herbert from Annuals and Antiquities, vol. II, pg. 776-777. However, it is noted, that Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) was not the Count of Vermandois, but rather his uncle was, Herbert IV, Count of Vermandois. It is also noted that the son of Herbertus Camerarius, Henry FitzHerbert (Henry the Treasurer) is missing from the Annals and Antiquities, but his grandson Herbert (Herbert FitzHenry) and great grandson Herbert  (Herbert FitzHerbert) are included.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Pedigree of the family of Herbert of Vermandois (Herbertus Camerarius) in Collections Historical & Archaeological Relating to Montgomeryshire, Vol. V, pages 158-159

memoryArtifacts.headline: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer)
memoryArtifacts.description: Herbert the Chamberlain (Herbertus Camerarius), in A Genealogical and Heraldic History of the Landed Gentry of Great Britain and Ireland, Vol. 1, pg. 613 [but missing the generation of Henry the Treasurer). The record skips to the son of Henry, Herbert FitzHenry (Herbert FitzHerbert I, and his son Herbert FitzHerbert (II).

nameMeanings.name: Walsh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, Dermot, John Patrick, Kieran, Declan, Eamonn, Colm, Donal, Seamus, Siobhan, Brennan.

English and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English walshe (Old English wælisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’. This form of the name was generally more common than Welsh , especially in Ireland, where it originally denoted a Welsh-speaking immigrant, in particular any of the Welshmen who arrived in the wake of Strongbow's Anglo-Norman invasion of 1170. In Ireland it is sometimes also a translation of Breathnach ‘Briton’.
English: in Yorkshire and Lancashire the name is sometimes a shortened form of   Walshaw  .

variants.name: Walshe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eoin, Murph.
English and Irish: variant of   Walsh  .

variants.name: McBratney
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breatnaigh ‘son of the Briton’ (referring to the Welsh of Strathclyde). Compare Walsh and Galbraith .

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Walsh was born in 1255, in Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Wales, her father, Sir John Walsh, was 9 and her mother, Mrs. John Walsh, was 10. She had at least 1 son with Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Walsh est née en 1255, à Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Sir John Walsh, avait 9 ans et sa mère, Mrs. John Walsh, avait 10 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Walsh 1255 in Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Walsh 9 und ihre Mutter Mrs. John Walsh 10. Sie hatte mit Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Walsh è nata il nel 1255 nel Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Galles, sua padre, Sir John Walsh, aveva 9 anni e sua madre, Mrs. John Walsh, aveva 10 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.

ja.Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Wales: ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアランロウエル
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Walshが1255年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアランロウエルで生まれた時，父Sir John Walshは9歳，母Mrs. John Walshは10歳であった。彼女はHerbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowellとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Walsh(이)가 1255년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 랜로월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Walsh(은)는 9세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. John Walsh(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Walsh nasceu em 1255, em Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Gales, seu pai, Sir John Walsh, tinha 9 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. John Walsh, tinha 10 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Walsh родилась в 1255 г. в местности лланллоуелл, монмоутшир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Sir John Walsh было 9 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. John Walsh, 10. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Walsh nació en 1255, en Llanllowell, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Sir John Walsh, tenía 9 años y su mamá, Mrs. John Walsh, tenía 10 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Herbert FitzPeter, Lord of Llanlowell.
